content:
Let the line be \(y=0\) and the big circle \(B\) have center \((0,2008)\).  A small circle of radius \(r_n\) tangent to the line has center \((x_n,r_n)\).  External tangency to \(B\) gives
\[
(x_n-0)^2+(r_n-2008)^2=(2008+r_n)^2
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
x_n^2=4\cdot2008\,r_n=8032\,r_n.
\]
External tangency between \(C_{n-1}\) (radius \(r_{n-1}\), center \((x_{n-1},r_{n-1})\)) and \(C_n\) gives
\[
(x_n-x_{n-1})^2+(r_n-r_{n-1})^2=(r_n+r_{n-1})^2
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
x_n-x_{n-1}=2\sqrt{r_n\,r_{n-1}}.
\]
Since \(x_n=\sqrt{8032\,r_n}\), set \(b_n=\sqrt{r_n}\).  The last condition becomes
\[
\sqrt{8032}\,b_n-\sqrt{8032}\,b_{n-1}=2\,b_n\,b_{n-1}
\;\Longrightarrow\;
\frac1{b_n}-\frac1{b_{n-1}}=\frac{2}{\sqrt{8032}}.
\]
Hence if \(u_n=1/b_n\),
\[
u_n=u_{n-1}-\frac{2}{\sqrt{8032}}, 
\quad u_1=\frac1{\sqrt{1}}=1.
\]
Thus
\[
u_n=1-(n-1)\frac{2}{\sqrt{8032}}.
\]
We need \(u_n>0\), i.e. 
\[
1-(n-1)\frac{2}{\sqrt{8032}}>0
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
n-1<\frac{\sqrt{8032}}2\approx44.818,
\]
so the largest integer \(n\) is \(n=45\).  Therefore the maximal number of circles is

\(\boxed{45}\).
